title: 10 best herbal decoctions for swollen feet
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for swollen feet are natural remedies made by steeping herbs in hot water, which helps to alleviate swelling, reduce pain, and promote relaxation. These decoctions work by increasing blood circulation, reducing inflammation, and relaxing muscles. Examples of herbal decoctions that help with swollen feet include peppermint tea, which cools the skin and reduces itching; chamomile tea, which calms the nerves and promotes relaxation; and ginger tea, which reduces inflammation and warms the skin. By using these herbal decoctions, people can improve their quality of life by reducing discomfort and improving mobility, allowing them to engage in daily activities with ease and confidence.

Chop the cleaned equisetum arvense into small pieces to increase the surface area for infusion.
Combine 1 cup of chopped equisetum arvense with 2 cups of water in a large saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer the decoction for 10 to 15 minutes then strain and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana decoctions helps with swollen feet because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The decoction's active compounds, such as tannins and flavonoids, help to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid retention in the feet. remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly with a fine mesh strainer.
Combine the dried plant material with 1 quart of water in a saucepan and bring it to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and use 1/4 to 1/2 cup as needed.
